deck.author: OpenAI Codex
deck.subject: Two-dimensional presentation sample
deck.title: Two-Dimensional Demo
deck.audience: slideotter maintainers and authors evaluating 2D deck behavior
deck.objective: Show how a presentation can keep a short horizontal story while storing optional depth beneath selected slides.
deck.constraints: Keep the core path understandable without detours. Put optional operational detail in vertical slides only.
deck.themeBrief: Calm product-workflow sample with green accents and high-contrast typography.
deck.outline:
1. Core path first - The audience can follow the horizontal path without optional detail.
2. Detours add depth - Vertical slides hold implementation notes and presenter backup.
3. Outputs stay intentional - Core exports remain concise while presentation mode can include detours.
4. Authoring rule - Add detours only when they support, not replace, the main story.
deck.structureSummary: A four-slide horizontal path with two optional vertical detours.

structurePlan.title: Vertical detours
structurePlan.summary: Slides 5-6 attach optional depth to slides 2 and 3.

slide-01.intent: Introduce the mental model for two-dimensional presentations.
slide-01.layoutHint: Cover slide with three compact cards that define horizontal, vertical, and export behavior.
slide-01.mustInclude: Core path first; optional depth lives below selected slides.
slide-01.notes: Start in presentation mode and use right arrow to advance on the core path.
slide-01.title: Core path, optional depth

slide-02.intent: Explain that horizontal navigation remains the primary story.
slide-02.layoutHint: Content slide with one strong claim and three supporting cards.
slide-02.mustInclude: The core path must remain understandable without detours.
slide-02.notes: Press down from this slide to show the authoring detail detour.
slide-02.title: Keep the main story horizontal

slide-03.intent: Explain how outputs treat two-dimensional decks.
slide-03.layoutHint: Content slide with export and presentation behavior separated into cards.
slide-03.mustInclude: Presentation mode includes detours; core exports stay concise by default.
slide-03.notes: Press down from this slide to show the export detail detour.
slide-03.title: Outputs stay intentional

slide-04.intent: Give authors a practical rule for adding detours.
slide-04.layoutHint: Summary slide with three next-step cards.
slide-04.mustInclude: Use detours for backup detail, not required narrative steps.
slide-04.notes: Close on the authoring rule and point to Slide Studio's add-detour flow.
slide-04.title: Use detours deliberately

slide-05.intent: Show the authoring details that sit below the main story slide.
slide-05.layoutHint: Quote or checklist slide that can be skipped in the spoken path.
slide-05.mustInclude: Detours are attached to a core slide and navigated vertically.
slide-05.notes: This slide is optional detail below slide 2.
slide-05.title: Detour: authoring detail

slide-06.intent: Clarify export behavior for two-dimensional decks.
slide-06.layoutHint: Checklist slide with core export and presentation mode contrasted.
slide-06.mustInclude: Core preview/export ignores detours unless a full-path option is requested later.
slide-06.notes: This slide is optional detail below slide 3.
slide-06.title: Detour: output detail
